post.summary: Create a SKU item
post.description:
Creates a new SKU item under the client account.

- `sku_id` is client-defined and becomes the primary identifier in the API.
- Enabling capabilities may trigger internal preparation; readiness is returned in `capabilities_readiness`.
- Returns the created SKU item.

400.description: The request is syntactically valid but cannot be processed (business validation failed).

401.description: Returned by API Gateway when API key or token is missing or invalid.

403.description: Returned by API Gateway when access is not allowed.

429.description: Returned by API Gateway when rate limits are exceeded.

409.description: SKU item with this sku_id already exists.

422.description: Returned when the request payload is structurally invalid or contains invalid field values. The response format follows FastAPI / Pydantic defaults for scheme validation errors but may contain specific details for custom fails like not yet supported functionality.

patch.description:
Replaces the entire `capabilities` block for the SKU item. The request body is treated as a full replacement: omitted fields are reset (to their default value, empty value, or null depending on the field).

Enabling or disabling capabilities may trigger internal preparation. Current readiness is returned in `capabilities_readiness`. Enabling, disabling, or changing requested image variants may trigger preparation. Readiness for newly enabled/changed capabilities may start as preparing.

patch.description:
Replaces the entire `merchandising` block for the SKU item. Note: this endpoint does not support partial updates. The request body is treated as a full replacement: omitted fields are reset (to their default value, empty value, or null depending on the field).

Updating merchandising attributes typically does not require the whole SKU item re-processing, but may affect downstream scenarios (e.g., recommendation flows).

get.summary: Get outfit recommendations for SKU item
get.description:
Returns outfit recommendations for the given SKU item.

- The requested SKU item is always included as the first element of `Outfit.sku_ids`.
- The response contains exactly `count` outfits.

This endpoint requires the Outfit Recommendations capability to be enabled and ready for the SKU item:
- `SkuItem.capabilities.outfit_recommendations == true`
- `SkuItem.capabilities_readiness.outfit_recommendations.status == "ready"`

If the capability is disabled or not ready, the request fails with status code 409.

parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: Optional user identifier used for personalization. If omitted, recommendations are non-personalized.
schema.title: User Id

parameters.description: Optional user identifier used for personalization. If omitted, recommendations are non-personalized.

schema.title: Response Get Outfit Recommendations Unified Api Recommendations Outfits  Sku Id  Get

post.summary: Create a flat-lay generation operation
post.description:
Starts an asynchronous operation to generate a flat-lay product image.

You must provide exactly one of:
- `product_info` (inline product data), or
- `sku_id` (generate using a previously created SKU).

When `sku_id` is used, the SKU must have the corresponding product-images capability enabled and ready.
Use `GET /sku_items/{sku_id}` to check readiness.

The response contains an operation ID. Poll `GET /flat_lay_operations/{operation_id}` until the operation completes and `generated_image` is available.

400.description: The request is syntactically valid but cannot be processed (business validation failed).

401.description: Returned by API Gateway when API key or token is missing or invalid.

403.description: Returned by API Gateway when access is not allowed.

429.description: Returned by API Gateway when rate limits are exceeded.

409.description: Flat-lay generation is not available for this SKU item (capability disabled or not ready).

422.description: Returned when the request payload is structurally invalid or contains invalid field values. The response format follows FastAPI / Pydantic defaults for scheme validation errors but may contain specific details for custom fails like not yet supported functionality.

get.summary: Get a flat-lay generation operation
get.description:
Returns the operation details, including its status and the generated image (if completed).

If the operation is still running or failed, `generated_image` may be absent.

post.summary: Start on-figure image generation operation.
post.description: Submits a request to generate product images featuring an on-figure model, based on the provided product data, model configuration, and desired image types. The operation may take time to complete depending on workload and priority. Returns an object containing `operation_id`, which can be used to track progress and retrieve results (see `/on_figure_operations/{operation_id}`).

post.summary: Regenerate specific on-figure product image types.
post.description: Submits a request to regenerate specific image types from a previous on-figure image generation. This is useful for refining selected views or adding new ones while preserving the original model appearance and product configuration. You must specify the `original_operation_id` and the list of `image_types` to regenerate. Optionally, you can override the number of image variants per type. Returns an object containing `operation_id`, which can be used to track progress and retrieve results (see `/on_figure_operations/{operation_id}`).

AbortReason.title: AbortReason
AbortReason.description:
Reasons why an operation may be aborted.

Some checks can be configured per client. Please contact the Aiuta team for details.

- `NO_PEOPLE_DETECTED`: No person was found in the input image.
- `TOO_MANY_PEOPLE_DETECTED`: Too many people were found in the input image.
- `CHILD_DETECTED`: A minor was detected in the input image.
- `INSUFFICIENT_TARGET_AREA`: The body area required for this garment is not visible enough in the input image.
- `INTERNAL_RESTRICTION`: The generated result cannot be returned due to internal restrictions.

AgeGroupType.title: AgeGroupType
AgeGroupType.description: Age group of the on-figure model used for image generation.

owner_type.description: Type of the image owner. It will be 'user' if the image was directly uploaded by the user or derived from a user upload (e.g., through a generation operation). Otherwise, it will be 'aiuta'. In this case the image is not associated with users' personal data and does not belong to them.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Expiration time of the image. The image may not be available after this time.

powered_by_sticker.description: Settings for the 'Powered by Aiuta' sticker feature

size_and_fit_disclaimer.description: Settings for the size and fit disclaimer feature

images_ttl_seconds.title: Images Ttl Seconds
images_ttl_seconds.description: Number of seconds during which uploaded and generated images are guaranteed to be stored

ApiError.title: ApiError
ApiError.description:
Error response returned for non-2xx responses.

`code` is a stable machine-readable identifier.
`message` is a human-readable description.
`details` may provide structured context (not guaranteed to be stable).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generated_images.title: Generated Images
generated_images.description: List of generated images. None means that the image is not ready yet or there was an error during generation

BackgroundImagesOperation.title: BackgroundImagesOperation
BackgroundImagesOperation.description: Model for background images generation operations

operation_id.title: Operation Id
operation_id.description: ID of the created background images generation operation.

details.title: Details
details.description: Additional information about the operation creation process.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Errors list happened during the operation creation process.

background_type.description: Type of background to apply in the generated image (e.g., arbitrary, transparent, reference-colored, or reference-image).

reference_color.title: Reference Color
reference_color.description: Optional reference color for background generation. Used only if `background_type` is set to 'reference_color'. The resulting background will not be a flat solid fill, but rather a blend of gradients and tones inspired by the specified color. The chosen color serves as the visual base or anchor. Must be a valid RGB hex code (e.g., '#80ff00').

reference_image_id.title: Reference Image Id
reference_image_id.description: ID of the uploaded image to use as a reference background. Used only if `background_type` is set to 'reference_image'.

reference_image.description: Resolved storage path of the reference background image. Set internally after resolving `reference_image_id`. Not provided by the client.

BackgroundSettings.title: BackgroundSettings
BackgroundSettings.description:
Configuration for generating the background of an image.

The background may be one of several types: arbitrary (default), transparent, reference-colored,
or reference-image-based. Depending on the selected `background_type`, additional fields may be required:

- If `background_type` is 'reference_color', a valid RGB hex color must be provided via `reference_color`.
  The background will be generated based on this color as a visual base, but not as a solid fill.
- If `background_type` is 'reference_image', an uploaded image ID must be provided via `reference_image_id`.
  The background will be generated based on this image. The `reference_image` field is resolved internally.

The model enforces consistency between the selected type and the presence or absence of these additional fields.

BackgroundType.title: BackgroundType
BackgroundType.description: Defines how the background will be generated (e.g., solid, transparent or reference-colored).

BodyShapeType.title: BodyShapeType
BodyShapeType.description: Body shape category of the on-figure model, used to reflect realistic garment fit.

batch_id.title: Batch Id
batch_id.description: ID of the batch to group multiple operations.

This attribute helps guide the generation of culturally representative facial features and appearance.

gratitude_message.title: Gratitude Message

image_type.description: Image type (flat-lay type and viewpoint) this result corresponds to.

generated_images.title: Generated Images
generated_images.description: List of generated image variants for the specified image type.

FlatLayGenerationResultItem.title: FlatLayGenerationResultItem
FlatLayGenerationResultItem.description:
Represents the generated output for a specific image type in a flat-lay product images generation operation.

Each item corresponds to a single combination of flat-lay type and viewpoint (i.e., an `ImageType`)
and includes the number of generated image variants as specified by the `number_of_generations` parameter
in the generation request.

flat_lay_type.description: Layout style of the flat-lay image (e.g., flat, ghost mannequin, detail view).

viewpoint.description: View direction for the image (e.g., front or back).

FlatLayImageType.title: FlatLayImageType
FlatLayImageType.description: Specifies a flat-lay product image style, defined by layout type and viewpoint.

FlatLayType.title: FlatLayType
FlatLayType.description:
Specifies the style of a flat-lay product image.
- `flat`: a traditional top-down flat-lay presentation of the item.
- `ghost_mannequin`: a simulated 3D look by removing the mannequin, preserving garment shape.
- `detail`: a close-up view highlighting textures, materials, or stitching.

FlatLayViewpointType.title: FlatLayViewpointType
FlatLayViewpointType.description: Direction from which the flat-lay image should be rendered.

product_info.description: Structured product data. See `SkuProductInfo` for details.

operation_priority.description: Specifies whether the operation should be processed online (real-time with automatic QA) or offline (batch with manual QA).

background_settings.description: Configuration for background generation. See `BackgroundSettings` for details on supported types and related fields.

output_image_size.description: Desired final dimensions of the output images. If not provided, a default internal size is used. This parameter guides the image normalization logic to produce outputs that match the requested width and height.

output_image_paddings.description: Desired padding around the generated object in the final images. Used by the image normalization logic to ensure consistent spacing around each output image. If omitted, padding will not be enforced and will simply reflect the naturally generated result.

image_type.description: Desired flat-lay product image type to generate. Refer to `FlatLayImageType`.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generated_image.description: Generation result for a flat-lay product image generation operation. May be absent if the operation is still in progress or encountered an error.

GenerateFlatLayImageOperation.title: GenerateFlatLayImageOperation
GenerateFlatLayImageOperation.description:
Represents a completed or in-progress operation for generating flat-lay product image.

This object combines the original generation request parameters with the result of the operation.
It allows tracking the output and progress of an image generation process using the same model configuration
and requested image type.

The operation may be incomplete or errored, in which case the `generated_image` may be missing.

product_info.description: Structured product data. See `SkuProductInfo` for details.

operation_priority.description: Specifies whether the operation should be processed online (real-time with automatic QA) or offline (batch with manual QA).

background_settings.description: Configuration for background generation. See `BackgroundSettings` for details on supported types and related fields.

output_image_size.description: Desired final dimensions of the output images. If not provided, a default internal size is used. This parameter guides the image normalization logic to produce outputs that match the requested width and height.

output_image_paddings.description: Desired padding around the generated object in the final images. Used by the image normalization logic to ensure consistent spacing around each output image. If omitted, padding will not be enforced and will simply reflect the naturally generated result.

image_type.description: Desired flat-lay product image type to generate. Refer to `FlatLayImageType`.

GenerateFlatLayImageRequest.title: GenerateFlatLayImageRequest
GenerateFlatLayImageRequest.description:
Request to generate a flat-lay product image for a clothing item.

The request specifies the desired flat-lay style (e.g., flat, ghost mannequin, detail)
and viewpoint (e.g., front, back). The image can be generated either from inline `product_info`
or from a previously created SKU via `sku_id`.

product_info.description: Structured product data. See `ProductInfo` for details.

operation_priority.description: Specifies whether the operation should be processed online (real-time) or offline (batch).

number_of_generations.title: Number Of Generations
number_of_generations.description: Number of output generated images of each type.

background_settings.description: Configuration for background generation. See `BackgroundSettings` for details on supported types and related fields.

output_image_size.description: Desired final dimensions of the output images. If not provided, a default internal size is used. This parameter guides the image normalization logic to produce outputs that match the requested width and height.

output_image_paddings.description: Desired padding around the generated object in the final images. Used by the image normalization logic to ensure consistent spacing around each output image. If omitted, padding will not be enforced and will simply reflect the naturally generated result.

image_types.title: Image Types
image_types.description: List of desired flat-lay product image types to generate. Refer to `ImageType`. `ImageType` values must be unique and at least one type must be specified.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generation_result.title: Generation Result
generation_result.description: Generation result for a flat-lay product images generation operation. Each list item corresponds to an `ImageType` in the same order as specified in the request. May be absent if the operation is still in progress or encountered an error.

GenerateFlatLayImagesOperation.title: GenerateFlatLayImagesOperation
GenerateFlatLayImagesOperation.description:
Represents a completed or in-progress operation for generating flat-lay product images.

This object combines the original generation request parameters with the result of the operation.
It allows tracking the output and progress of an image generation process using the same model configuration
and requested image types.

The operation may be incomplete or errored, in which case the `generation_result` may be missing.

product_info.description: Structured product data. See `ProductInfo` for details.

operation_priority.description: Specifies whether the operation should be processed online (real-time) or offline (batch).

number_of_generations.title: Number Of Generations
number_of_generations.description: Number of output generated images of each type.

background_settings.description: Configuration for background generation. See `BackgroundSettings` for details on supported types and related fields.

output_image_size.description: Desired final dimensions of the output images. If not provided, a default internal size is used. This parameter guides the image normalization logic to produce outputs that match the requested width and height.

output_image_paddings.description: Desired padding around the generated object in the final images. Used by the image normalization logic to ensure consistent spacing around each output image. If omitted, padding will not be enforced and will simply reflect the naturally generated result.

image_types.title: Image Types
image_types.description: List of desired flat-lay product image types to generate. Refer to `ImageType`. `ImageType` values must be unique and at least one type must be specified.

GenerateFlatLayImagesRequest.title: GenerateFlatLayImagesRequest
GenerateFlatLayImagesRequest.description:
Request to generate flat-lay product images for a given clothing item.

This request allows clients to specify desired flat-lay presentation styles
(e.g., ghost mannequin, detail shot, or classic flat-lay) and viewpoints (e.g., front, back).
It is intended for generating clean, studio-style visuals of products laid flat,
commonly used in e-commerce product galleries and catalogs.

product_info.description: Structured product data. See `ProductInfo` for details.

operation_priority.description: Specifies whether the operation should be processed online (real-time) or offline (batch).

number_of_generations.title: Number Of Generations
number_of_generations.description: Number of output generated images of each type.

background_settings.description: Configuration for background generation. See `BackgroundSettings` for details on supported types and related fields.

output_image_size.description: Desired final dimensions of the output images. If not provided, a default internal size is used. This parameter guides the image normalization logic to produce outputs that match the requested width and height.

output_image_paddings.description: Desired padding around the generated object in the final images. Used by the image normalization logic to ensure consistent spacing around each output image. If omitted, padding will not be enforced and will simply reflect the naturally generated result.

image_types.title: Image Types
image_types.description: List of desired on-figure product image types to generate. Refer to `ImageType`. `ImageType` values must be unique and at least one type must be specified.

on_figure_model_preset_id.title: On Figure Model Preset Id
on_figure_model_preset_id.description: ID of a predefined model preset. Either on_figure_model_preset_id or on_figure_model_settings (but not both) must be specified.

on_figure_model_settings.description: Manually set on-figure model settings. Either on_figure_model_preset_id or on_figure_model_settings (but not both) must be specified.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generation_result.title: Generation Result
generation_result.description: Generation result for an on-figure product images generation operation. Each list item corresponds to an `ImageType` in the same order as specified in the request. May be absent if the operation is still in progress or encountered an error.

GenerateOnFigureImagesOperation.title: GenerateOnFigureImagesOperation
GenerateOnFigureImagesOperation.description:
Represents a completed or in-progress operation for generating on-figure product images.

This object combines the original generation request parameters with the result of the operation.
It allows tracking the output and progress of an image generation process using the same model configuration
and requested image types.

The operation may be incomplete or errored, in which case the `generation_result` may be missing.

product_info.description: Structured product data. See `ProductInfo` for details.

operation_priority.description: Specifies whether the operation should be processed online (real-time) or offline (batch).

number_of_generations.title: Number Of Generations
number_of_generations.description: Number of output generated images of each type.

background_settings.description: Configuration for background generation. See `BackgroundSettings` for details on supported types and related fields.

output_image_size.description: Desired final dimensions of the output images. If not provided, a default internal size is used. This parameter guides the image normalization logic to produce outputs that match the requested width and height.

output_image_paddings.description: Desired padding around the generated object in the final images. Used by the image normalization logic to ensure consistent spacing around each output image. If omitted, padding will not be enforced and will simply reflect the naturally generated result.

image_types.title: Image Types
image_types.description: List of desired on-figure product image types to generate. Refer to `ImageType`. `ImageType` values must be unique and at least one type must be specified.

on_figure_model_preset_id.title: On Figure Model Preset Id
on_figure_model_preset_id.description: ID of a predefined model preset. Either on_figure_model_preset_id or on_figure_model_settings (but not both) must be specified.

on_figure_model_settings.description: Manually set on-figure model settings. Either on_figure_model_preset_id or on_figure_model_settings (but not both) must be specified.

GenerateOnFigureImagesRequest.title: GenerateOnFigureImagesRequest
GenerateOnFigureImagesRequest.description:
Request to generate product images featuring on-figure models for a given clothing item.

This request supports detailed customization of the model's appearance
(e.g., gender, age group, body shape, skin tone)
and allows specifying the desired types of images to generate (e.g., front, angled, close-up).
It is intended for generating photorealistic visuals of products worn by human models,
typically for use on product detail pages.

GeneratedBackgroundImage.title: GeneratedBackgroundImage
GeneratedBackgroundImage.description: External model for generated background image result

GeneratedDiversityImage.title: GeneratedDiversityImage
GeneratedDiversityImage.description: Model for generated diversity image result

GeneratedMannequinImage.title: GeneratedMannequinImage
GeneratedMannequinImage.description: External model for generated mannequin image result

owner_type.description: Type of the image owner. It will be 'user' if the image was directly uploaded by the user or derived from a user upload (e.g., through a generation operation). Otherwise, it will be 'aiuta'. In this case the image is not associated with users' personal data and does not belong to them.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Expiration time of the image. The image may not be available after this time.

GeneratedSkuImage.title: GeneratedSkuImage
GeneratedSkuImage.description: External model for generated SKU image result

GeneratedStyleTryOnImage.title: GeneratedStyleTryOnImage
GeneratedStyleTryOnImage.description: External model for generated Style try on image result

HairColorType.title: HairColorType
HairColorType.description: Color of the model's hair, used for visual customization.

HairLengthType.title: HairLengthType
HairLengthType.description: Length category of the model's hair for appearance customization.

HairStyleType.title: HairStyleType
HairStyleType.description: Style or texture of the model's hair.

HeightType.title: HeightType
HeightType.description: Categorical height classification used for rendering model proportions.

top.title: Top
top.description: Padding above the image content, in pixels

bottom.title: Bottom
bottom.description: Padding below the image content, in pixels

left.title: Left
left.description: Padding to the left of the image content, in pixels

right.title: Right
right.description: Padding to the right of the image content, in pixels

ImagePaddings.title: ImagePaddings
ImagePaddings.description: Padding around the image content, specified in pixels for each side

width.title: Width
width.description: Width of the image in pixels

height.title: Height
height.description: Height of the image in pixels

category.title: Category
category.description: Category name of the try-on models

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generated_images.title: Generated Images
generated_images.description: List of generated images. None means that the image is not ready yet or there was an error during generation

MannequinImagesOperation.title: MannequinImagesOperation
MannequinImagesOperation.description: Model for mannequin images generation operations

operation_id.title: Operation Id
operation_id.description: ID of the created mannequin images generation operation.

details.title: Details
details.description: Additional information about the operation creation process.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Errors list happened during the operation creation process.

ObjectStoragePath.title: ObjectStoragePath
ObjectStoragePath.description: Common class for a stored object

readiness.description: Preparation status for this image variant.

OffFigureProductImagesCapabilityReadines.title: OffFigureProductImagesCapabilityReadinessItem
OffFigureProductImagesCapabilityReadines.description: Readiness information for a specific requested off-figure product image variant.

image_type.description: Image type (viewpoint and pose) this result corresponds to.

generated_images.title: Generated Images
generated_images.description: List of generated image variants for the specified image type.

OnFigureGenerationResultItem.title: OnFigureGenerationResultItem
OnFigureGenerationResultItem.description:
Represents the generated output for a specific image type in an on-figure product images generation operation.

Each item corresponds to a single combination of viewpoint and pose (i.e., an `ImageType`)
and includes the number of generated image variants as specified by the `number_of_generations` parameter
in the generation request.

viewpoint.description: Camera angle or viewpoint of the image.

pose.description: Model pose to be shown in the image.

OnFigureImageType.title: OnFigureImageType
OnFigureImageType.description:
Specifies an on-figure product image style, defined by viewpoint and model pose.

This object is used to specify the desired composition and perspective of a product image.

preset_id.title: Preset Id
preset_id.description: ID of a predefined model preset.

sample_image.title: Sample Image
sample_image.description: URL of a sample image showing how the generated model will look.

settings.description: Detailed model settings used in this on-figure model preset.

OnFigureModelPresetDescription.title: OnFigureModelPresetDescription
OnFigureModelPresetDescription.description:
Describes a predefined on-figure model preset for product image generation.

Each preset includes a unique identifier, a sample image demonstrating the model's general appearance,
and a full set of configuration settings (e.g., gender, body shape, skin tone).

Presets allow users to apply common predefined configurations without manually specifying all parameters.
Note that the exact visual appearance (e.g., specific facial features) may differ from the sample image,
as presets define parameter templates rather than fixed model identities.

gender.description: Gender of the model. This is the only required setting.

ethnicity.description: Ethnic or regional background of the model (e.g., 'european', 'asian', 'latino'). Used to influence visual traits such as facial structure and hair type.

skin_tone.description: Approximate skin tone of the model, ranging from pale white to black. Used to guide realistic and inclusive visual representation.

body_shape.description: Overall body shape of the model (e.g., 'thin', 'muscular', 'plus_sized', 'regular'), used to reflect garment fit and silhouette.

age_group.description: Life stage of the model (e.g., 'child', 'teen', 'young_adult', 'senior_adult'), influencing facial features and body proportions.

has_beard.title: Has Beard
has_beard.description: Whether the model has a beard.

has_tattoo.title: Has Tattoo
has_tattoo.description: Whether the model has visible tattoos.

OnFigureModelSettings.title: OnFigureModelSettings
OnFigureModelSettings.description:
Detailed configuration for an on-figure model used to define the appearance of a model in product image generation.
These settings influence how the model will be rendered in the generated images.

OnFigurePoseType.title: OnFigurePoseType
OnFigurePoseType.description: Describes the posture or pose of the model in the image.

readiness.description: Preparation status for this image variant.

OnFigureProductImagesCapabilityReadiness.title: OnFigureProductImagesCapabilityReadinessItem
OnFigureProductImagesCapabilityReadiness.description: Readiness information for a specific requested on-figure product image variant.

OnFigureViewpointType.title: OnFigureViewpointType
OnFigureViewpointType.description: Camera angle or view direction for the image (e.g., front, side, back).

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: recurring - the delay will be repeated a specified repeat number of times, infinite - the delay will repeat infinitely. If the sequence does not contain an infinite value and ends before a successful result is obtained, the operation will fail with a timeout error, the user will be shown an “something went wrong” error

OperationPriority.title: OperationPriority
OperationPriority.description:
Defines the operation priority.

- `online`: processed as quickly as possible for real-time, user-facing requests.
  Typically uses automatic AI-based quality assurance (QA).
- `offline`: suited for batch processing where manual QA may be used to achieve higher quality.

OwnerType.title: OwnerType
OwnerType.description:
Type of the image owner. It will be 'user' if the image was directly uploaded by the user or
derived from a user upload (e.g., through a generation operation). Otherwise, it will be 'aiuta'.
In this case the image is not associated with users' personal data and does not belong to them.

url_ios.title: Url Ios
url_ios.description: URL where the sticker leads to on iOS

url_android.title: Url Android
url_android.description: URL where the sticker leads to on Android

is_visible.title: Is Visible
is_visible.description: Whether the sticker is visible or not

id.title: Id
id.description: Optional client-defined identifier for the product. Not used internally by the system, but can be included in requests to help clients correlate operation results with their own product records.

image_urls.title: Image Urls
image_urls.description: List of up to 20 image URLs representing the product. Must not be used together with `uploaded_image_ids` - only one of the two fields may be provided. If specified in an input request, the images will be downloaded and stored internally. In operation status or result responses, this field is always present and contains internal image URLs, regardless of whether the original input used uploaded IDs or external URLs. If the images have not yet been downloaded - or if the download failed - this list may be empty.

uploaded_image_ids.title: Uploaded Image Ids
uploaded_image_ids.description: List of 1 to 20 IDs of previously uploaded product images. Must not be used together with `image_urls` - only one of the two fields may be provided. This field is accepted in input requests only and will never appear in operation status or result responses. In such responses, internally stored image URLs corresponding to these IDs will be returned via the `image_urls` field of the `ProductInfo` object.

category.title: Category
category.description: Category of the item. Preferably use a structured taxonomy such as 'Apparel & Accessories > Clothing > Dresses' or 'Home > Women > Dresses > Maxi Dresses'. Refer to the Google product category list as a good example of clear, structured categorization: https://support.google.com/merchants/answer/6324436

ProductInfo.title: ProductInfo
ProductInfo.description:
Describes the product for which images will be generated.

This includes the product images along with optional metadata such as category, title, and description.
Providing structured and accurate product information helps improve the relevance and realism
of the generated visuals.

original_operation_id.title: Original Operation Id
original_operation_id.description: ID of the original image generation operation whose configuration and model appearance will be reused. This can also be the ID of a previous regeneration operation, as it internally stores the required parameters.

number_of_generations.title: Number Of Generations
number_of_generations.description: Number of output images to generate for each specified type. If provided, this overrides the `number_of_generations` value from the original generation request. If omitted, the number of images will match the original request's setting.

image_types.title: Image Types
image_types.description: List of on-figure product image types to regenerate. `ImageType` values must be unique and at least one type must be specified. It can also be used to generate images of the types which were not specified initially. The same model appearance will be used to maintain consistency of the whole product images set.

RegenerateOnFigureImagesRequest.title: RegenerateOnFigureImagesRequest
RegenerateOnFigureImagesRequest.description:
Request to regenerate specific product image types from a previously submitted generation operation.

This request allows clients to regenerate selected image types (e.g., only the on-model front view)
using the same configuration (e.g., product details and model settings) as a previous operation.
It is particularly useful for refining or correcting specific outputs (or even add images of new types to the set)
without rerunning the entire generation pipeline, while preserving the on-figure model's appearance.

photo_upload.title: Photo Upload
photo_upload.description: Number of retries for photo preparation/compressing/uploading

operation_start.title: Operation Start
operation_start.description: Number of network request retries, has no effect if the server responds with error

operation_status.title: Operation Status
operation_status.description: Number of network request retries per loop, if the server responded, the number of retries will reset in the next loop after the delay in the operation_sequence

result_download.title: Result Download
result_download.description: Number of attempts to load the generated image

SegmentationPreset.title: SegmentationPreset
SegmentationPreset.description: External model for a preset of segmentation settings

ShopifyMannequinImagesOperationParameter.title: ShopifyMannequinImagesOperationParameters
ShopifyMannequinImagesOperationParameter.description: Parameters for mannequin images generation operations

SkinToneType.title: SkinToneType
SkinToneType.description:
Represents the skin tone of the on-figure model for image generation.

These values help guide realistic rendering of the model's appearance across a diverse range of skin tones.
The classification aims to be inclusive while maintaining practical distinctions for visual generation tasks.

SkuAvailability.title: SkuAvailability
SkuAvailability.description:
Commercial availability status of the SKU in the client's catalog.

Values follow Google Merchant Center product data specification for the `availability` attribute:
https://support.google.com/merchants/answer/6324448

try_on.title: Try On
try_on.description: Enables or disables Virtual Try-On for this SKU. Preparation progress is exposed via `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.try_on`.

outfit_recommendations.title: Outfit Recommendations
outfit_recommendations.description: Enables or disables Outfit Recommendations for this SKU. When enabled, the SKU may be used in recommendation flows (subject to SKU availability in `SkuItem.merchandising`). Preparation progress is exposed via `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.outfit_recommendations`.

size_and_fit.title: Size And Fit
size_and_fit.description: Enables or disables Size&Fit capability for this SKU. Preparation progress is exposed via `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.size_and_fit`.

off_figure_product_images.title: Off Figure Product Images
off_figure_product_images.description: List of requested off-figure product image variants to generate for this SKU. An empty list means that no off-figure product image variants are requested. Each requested variant is prepared independently; see `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.off_figure_product_images`.

on_figure_product_images.title: On Figure Product Images
on_figure_product_images.description: List of requested on-figure product image variants to generate for this SKU. An empty list means that no on-figure product image variants are requested. Each requested variant is prepared independently; see `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.on_figure_product_images`.

SkuCapabilities-Input.title: SkuCapabilities
SkuCapabilities-Input.description:
Declares which features are enabled for this SKU.

For product image generation, capabilities are expressed as an explicit list of requested image variants.
The internal preparation process may depend on the specific requested image variants.
Each requested variant has a corresponding readiness entry.

try_on.title: Try On
try_on.description: Enables or disables Virtual Try-On for this SKU. Preparation progress is exposed via `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.try_on`.

outfit_recommendations.title: Outfit Recommendations
outfit_recommendations.description: Enables or disables Outfit Recommendations for this SKU. When enabled, the SKU may be used in recommendation flows (subject to SKU availability in `SkuItem.merchandising`). Preparation progress is exposed via `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.outfit_recommendations`.

size_and_fit.title: Size And Fit
size_and_fit.description: Enables or disables Size&Fit capability for this SKU. Preparation progress is exposed via `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.size_and_fit`.

off_figure_product_images.title: Off Figure Product Images
off_figure_product_images.description: List of requested off-figure product image variants to generate for this SKU. An empty list means that no off-figure product image variants are requested. Each requested variant is prepared independently; see `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.off_figure_product_images`.

on_figure_product_images.title: On Figure Product Images
on_figure_product_images.description: List of requested on-figure product image variants to generate for this SKU. An empty list means that no on-figure product image variants are requested. Each requested variant is prepared independently; see `SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.on_figure_product_images`.

SkuCapabilities-Output.title: SkuCapabilities
SkuCapabilities-Output.description:
Declares which features are enabled for this SKU.

For product image generation, capabilities are expressed as an explicit list of requested image variants.
The internal preparation process may depend on the specific requested image variants.
Each requested variant has a corresponding readiness entry.

try_on.description: Preparation status of Virtual Try-On for this SKU.

outfit_recommendations.description: Preparation status of Outfit Recommendations for this SKU.

size_and_fit.description: Preparation status of Size&Fit for this SKU.

off_figure_product_images.title: Off Figure Product Images
off_figure_product_images.description: Preparation status per requested off-figure product image variant.

on_figure_product_images.title: On Figure Product Images
on_figure_product_images.description: Preparation status per requested on-figure product image variant.

SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.title: SkuCapabilitiesReadiness
SkuCapabilitiesReadiness.description:
Current preparation status for each capability of the SKU.

For product image capabilities, readiness is reported per requested image variant.
The readiness lists are expected to correspond 1:1 to the requested variants in `SkuCapabilities`.
Each readiness list contains at most one entry per requested variant.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last update to this capability readiness. Reflects the most recent change in status or related metadata.

status.description: Current readiness status of the capability for this SKU.

error_message.title: Error Message
error_message.description: Optional human-readable error message if preparation failed. Intended for debugging and support; the message format is not guaranteed to be stable.

SkuCapabilityReadiness.title: SkuCapabilityReadiness
SkuCapabilityReadiness.description: Readiness state of a single capability, including optional error details.

SkuCapabilityReadinessStatus.title: SkuCapabilityReadinessStatus
SkuCapabilityReadinessStatus.description:
Indicates the current preparation status of a capability for the SKU.

Typical semantics:
- `absent`: the capability is not enabled or not requested.
- `preparing`: preparation is currently in progress.
- `ready`: preparation completed successfully and the capability can be used.
- `rejected`: preparation was completed but the result cannot be used due to quality checks or policy constraints.
- `failed`: preparation failed; see `error_message` for details.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique ID for the SKUs catalog.

SkuCatalogsList.title: SkuCatalogsList
SkuCatalogsList.description: List of SKU catalogs with pagination

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generated_images.title: Generated Images
generated_images.description: List of generated images. The list can be empty if images are not yet ready, there was an error during generation or image(s) already expired and were deleted

SkuImagesOperation.title: SkuImagesOperation
SkuImagesOperation.description: Model for SKU images generation operations

sku_id.title: Sku Id
sku_id.description: SKU item ID. Either 'sku_id' or 'sku_ids' must be specified. If you want to make a try-on with multiple SKU items (outfit's try-on), use 'sku_ids' instead

sku_ids.title: Sku Ids
sku_ids.description: List of SKU IDs. Either 'sku_id' or 'sku_ids' must be specified. This field must be used for outfit's try-on with multiple SKU items. It also can be used for a single-item try-on (you can specified just one item here). 1 or 2 items are allowed at the moment

enhanced_quality.title: Enhanced Quality
enhanced_quality.description: Experimental flag for enhanced quality image generation (for internal use only)

hijab_protection.title: Hijab Protection
hijab_protection.description: Experimental flag to protect hijab from regeneration

model_type.title: Model Type
model_type.description: Model type override (for internal use only)

operation_target.title: Operation Target
operation_target.description: Operation target override (for internal use only)

operation_id.title: Operation Id
operation_id.description: ID of the created SKU images generation operation.

details.title: Details
details.description: Additional information about the operation creation process.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Errors list happened during the operation processing.

SkuItemsList.title: SkuItemsList
SkuItemsList.description: List of SKU items with pagination

availability.description: Current catalog availability of the SKU (e.g., in stock, out of stock). May influence outfit recommendation results and other business logic. Uses Google Merchant Center availability values.

SkuMerchandising.title: SkuMerchandising
SkuMerchandising.description:
Commercial attributes that affect how the SKU is used in downstream scenarios (e.g., recommendations).
These fields typically do not describe the product itself, but describe its sellability and catalog state.

image_urls.title: Image Urls
image_urls.description: List of up to 20 image URLs representing the product. Must not be used together with `uploaded_image_ids` - only one of the two fields may be provided. If specified in an input request, the images will be downloaded and stored internally. In operation status or result responses, this field is always present and contains internal image URLs, regardless of whether the original input used uploaded IDs or external URLs. If the images have not yet been downloaded - or if the download failed - this list may be empty.

uploaded_image_ids.title: Uploaded Image Ids
uploaded_image_ids.description: List of 1 to 20 IDs of previously uploaded product images. Must not be used together with `image_urls` - only one of the two fields may be provided. This field is accepted in input requests only and will never appear in operation status or result responses. In such responses, internally stored image URLs corresponding to these IDs will be returned via the `image_urls` field of the `SkuProductInfo` object.

category.title: Category
category.description: Category of the item. Preferably use a structured taxonomy such as 'Apparel & Accessories > Clothing > Dresses' or 'Home > Women > Dresses > Maxi Dresses'. Refer to the Google product category list as a good example of clear, structured categorization: https://support.google.com/merchants/answer/6324436

store_url.title: Store Url
store_url.description: URL of the product page in the online store.

gender.description: Product gender. Uses Google Merchant Center gender values.

age_group.description: Product age group. Uses Google Merchant Center age_group values.

extra.title: Extra
extra.description: Client-specific product attributes stored and returned as-is. Values must be JSON-serializable. No schema is enforced by the API. The platform may use these attributes only for client-specific logic agreed during integration.

SkuProductInfo.title: SkuProductInfo
SkuProductInfo.description:
Describes the product represented by this SKU item.

This includes the product images along with optional metadata such as category, title, and description.

Most fields are aligned with Google Merchant Center product data specification
(https://support.google.com/merchants/answer/7052112) and use the same semantics where applicable.

Providing structured and accurate product information helps improve the relevance and realism
of the generated visuals.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generated_images.title: Generated Images
generated_images.description: List of generated images. The list can be empty if images are not yet ready, there was an error during generation or image(s) already expired and were deleted

SkuTryOnOperation.title: SkuTryOnOperation
SkuTryOnOperation.description: Model for SKU try-on generation operations

sku_id.title: Sku Id
sku_id.description: SKU item ID. Either 'sku_id' or 'sku_ids' must be specified. If you want to make a try-on with multiple SKU items (outfit's try-on), use 'sku_ids' instead.

sku_ids.title: Sku Ids
sku_ids.description: List of SKU IDs. Either 'sku_id' or 'sku_ids' must be specified. This field must be used for outfit's try-on with multiple SKU items. It also can be used for a single-item try-on (you can specified just one item here). 1 or 2 items are allowed at the moment.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error details if the operation was failed. None means that there were no errors.

generated_images.title: Generated Images
generated_images.description: List of generated images. None means that the image is not ready yet or there was an error during generation

StyleTryOnOperation.title: StyleTryOnOperation
StyleTryOnOperation.description: Model for Style try on operations

segmentation_preset_id.title: Segmentation Preset Id
segmentation_preset_id.description: Id of segmentation preset to use

reference_images_ids.title: Reference Images Ids
reference_images_ids.description: List of reference images IDs to use for the generation

StyleTryOnRequest.title: StyleTryOnRequest
StyleTryOnRequest.description: Create Style try on operation parameters

operation_id.title: Operation Id
operation_id.description: ID of the created Style try on operation.

details.title: Details

fashion_external_api_backend__app__schem.title: CreateSkuItemRequest
fashion_external_api_backend__app__schem.description:
Request payload for creating a new SKU item in the platform.

The client provides a stable `sku_id` that becomes the primary identifier of the SKU item in this API.
The SKU is created with structured `product_info` and an initial set of enabled `capabilities`.
Enabling capabilities may trigger internal preparation.
Readiness for each capability is reported in the resulting `SkuItem.capabilities_readiness`.

Notes:
- `sku_id` must be unique within the client account and cannot be changed after creation.
- `internal_id` is assigned by the platform and is not provided in this request.
- If `capabilities` is omitted, all capabilities are disabled by default.
- If merchandising is omitted (or provided as an empty object), availability defaults to `in_stock`.

pydantic_schemes__external_api__models__.title: SkuAgeGroup
pydantic_schemes__external_api__models__.description:
Target age group for the SKU (if applicable).

Values follow Google Merchant Center product data specification for the `age_group` attribute:
https://support.google.com/merchants/answer/6324463

pydantic_schemes__external_api__models__.description:
Target gender presentation for the SKU (if applicable).

Values follow Google Merchant Center product data specification for the `gender` attribute:
https://support.google.com/merchants/answer/6324479

internal_id.title: Internal Id
internal_id.description: Platform internal identifier. Provided for debugging/support purposes only.

sku_id.title: Sku Id
sku_id.description: Client-defined identifier of the SKU item. Used as the primary identifier in the API (path and references). Must be unique within the client account. Immutable after creation.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp when the SKU item was created. Set by the platform and immutable.

product_info.description: Structured product data used by the platform to process the SKU.

capabilities.description: Enabled capabilities and requested product image variants.

capabilities_readiness.description: Operational readiness status (and error message if available) for each capability (absent/preparing/ready/failed).

merchandising.description: Merchandising attributes such as stock availability that affect business logic.

pydantic_schemes__external_api__models__.title: SkuItem
pydantic_schemes__external_api__models__.description:
A SKU item registered in the platform.
Contains structured product information, enabled features (capabilities), and readiness status for each capability.
Updating SKU data or capabilities may trigger invalidation and re-processing of dependent features
(so readiness status may change).

Operations can only be requested when the corresponding capability has status `ready`:
- Virtual Try-On requires `capabilities_readiness.try_on.status == "ready"`.
- Outfit Recommendations requires `capabilities_readiness.outfit_recommendations.status == "ready"`.
- Product Images require the readiness status to be `"ready"` for the specific requested image variant.

User ID for gateway auth bypass.description: User ID for gateway auth bypass
User ID for gateway auth bypass.name: x-user-id

tags.name: data
tags.description: Input and generated images management - upload, retrieval and deletion

tags.name: sku_items
tags.description:
Manage SKU items in the platform.

## Asynchronous preparation
Creating or updating a SKU may trigger asynchronous internal preparation (e.g., processing product assets or preparing enabled capabilities).

Preparation progress is exposed via `SkuItem.capabilities_readiness`.
Readiness may transition over time (e.g., `preparing -> ready`), and may return to `preparing` after updates if affected assets/capabilities need re-processing.

Clients should poll `GET /sku_items/{sku_id}` until the required capability (or image variant) is `ready` before calling endpoints that depend on it. A capability (or image variant) is usable only when its readiness status is ready.

tags.name: recommendations
tags.description:
Recommendation endpoints.

## Capability and readiness
Some recommendation endpoints require the corresponding capability to be enabled and ready for a SKU item.

Outfit recommendations require:
- `SkuItem.capabilities.outfit_recommendations == true`
- `SkuItem.capabilities_readiness.outfit_recommendations.status == "ready"`

Clients should poll `GET /sku_items/{sku_id}` until readiness is `ready` before requesting recommendations.
